goal: Land the three G6 (Platform) external-binary plugin manifests for Phase 3 Week 10 — `plugins/platform/github/plugin.toml` (P3-W10-F05), `plugins/platform/git/plugin.toml` (P3-W10-F06), and `plugins/platform/filesystem/plugin.toml` (P3-W10-F07) — plus their devtools install hints, an end-to-end `crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g6_plugin_manifests.rs` integration test exercising real `PluginManager::health_check_with_timeout` round-trips for each, and replacements for the TODO-stub acceptance scripts at `scripts/verify/P3-W10-F05.sh`, `P3-W10-F06.sh`, `P3-W10-F07.sh` that pair an MCP `tools/list` smoke with a feature-specific tool-level smoke. Direct port of the WO-0074 G5 paired-manifest template (Context7 + Repomix) extended to a tri-manifest shape for the official `@modelcontextprotocol/servers` upstream packages: GitHub MCP (npx `@modelcontextprotocol/server-github`), Git MCP (uvx `mcp-server-git`), Filesystem MCP (npx `@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem`). Establishes the new `platform.*` capability namespace under `plugins/platform/` (parallel to `context.*`, `knowledge.*`, `architecture.*`, `search.*`, `navigate.*`). Per WO-0074 lesson §planner: `G5 → G6/G7/G8 paired-manifest template port — apply same shape to G6..G8`.
plan_summary: Mirror the WO-0074 G5 paired-manifest precedent verbatim, retargeted from Context to Platform group and scaled from 2 to 3 features. Three stdio MCP servers — GitHub MCP, Git MCP, Filesystem MCP — get hand-authored TOML manifests under a new `plugins/platform/<name>/plugin.toml` directory tree, each pinned to a verified-by-executor immutable upstream tag with `transport.type = "stdio"` and a documented `capabilities.provides` list under the `platform.*` namespace. Three devtools install scripts (`scripts/devtools/install-github-mcp.sh`, `install-git-mcp.sh`, `install-filesystem-mcp.sh`) mirror the `install-context7-mcp.sh` shape verbatim — informational-only, idempotent, optional warm-up via `--help`. A new `crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g6_plugin_manifests.rs` integration test file declares one `mod g6_plugin_manifests { ... }` at file root with three `#[tokio::test] async fn` health-check tests — substring selectors `g6_plugin_manifests::github_manifest_health_check`, `g6_plugin_manifests::git_manifest_health_check`, `g6_plugin_manifests::filesystem_manifest_health_check` MUST resolve cleanly per DEC-0007 + WO-0069/0074 lessons §"For planner: module-root placement". Three `scripts/verify/P3-W10-F0{5,6,7}.sh` scripts replace the TODO stubs and run the targeted `cargo test` selector plus a tool-level smoke (gated by `UCIL_SKIP_PLATFORM_PLUGIN_E2E` per the WO-0074 `UCIL_SKIP_CONTEXT_PLUGIN_E2E` precedent, defaulting to RUN). The M1/M2 mutation contract is pre-baked verbatim from WO-0074 §8/§9: M1 poisons `transport.command` for each manifest in turn (3× M1), M2 replaces the expected canonical tool name in the test file (3× M2 — executor MUST confirm exact tool names from live `tools/list` BEFORE choosing the literal, per WO-0074 lesson §executor: `DON'T translate live tools/list names to snake_case when they ship kebab-case`). No daemon source changes; no `crates/ucil-daemon/src/**` edits permitted. F05 GitHub MCP smoke gates the `tools/call` invocation behind `GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN` env presence per the WO-0069 Mem0 API-key short-circuit precedent — `tools/list` is unconditional load-bearing; the `tools/call` for repository listing requires auth and falls back to `[SKIP]` exit 0 if the env is unset.
scope_in: 1. NEW plugin manifest at `plugins/platform/github/plugin.toml` for the official `@modelcontextprotocol/server-github` upstream MCP server. Required keys: `[plugin] name="github" category="platform" priority="P0" version="<semver>"`; `[transport] type="stdio" command="npx" args=["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-github@<pinned-tag>"]`; `[capabilities] provides=["platform.github.list_repositories", "platform.github.list_pull_requests", "platform.github.get_workflow_status"]` (executor: confirm exact tool names by spawning the live binary and capturing the canonical `tools/list` response BEFORE writing the literal — prefer kebab-case-as-emitted-by-upstream over snake-case translation per WO-0074 lesson §executor #1; the master-plan §3.1 vocabulary `list_repositories` etc. is the capability NAME independent of the upstream's literal tool name); `[lifecycle] hot_cold=true idle_timeout_minutes=10` mirrors the codegraphcontext/Context7 pattern. Top-of-file rustdoc comment must document: source URL (https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers), npm package name (`@modelcontextprotocol/server-github`), pinned tag, binary launch command, advertised tools (full live list in the comment per WO-0069/0074 precedent — capture from a live `tools/list` invocation), transport type, API key requirements (`GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN` env var REQUIRED for the API-backed `tools/call` operations; `tools/list` works without auth and returns the static tool catalog regardless of token presence — document this verbatim for the verifier).

2. NEW plugin manifest at `plugins/platform/git/plugin.toml` for the official `mcp-server-git` upstream MCP server (Python; uvx-launched). Required keys: `[plugin] name="git" category="platform" priority="P0" version="<semver>"`; `[transport] type="stdio" command="uvx" args=["mcp-server-git@<pinned-tag>", "--repository", "<placeholder>"]` — NOTE on the `--repository` placeholder: the Git MCP server requires a `--repository` CLI flag pointing to an absolute git-repo path. The manifest `args` field SHOULD carry a placeholder token (e.g., `"${UCIL_GIT_MCP_REPO}"` or a documented sentinel) that the test/verify-script substitutes per-invocation. Executor: read the upstream `mcp-server-git --help` output, confirm the exact CLI flag spelling (it may also accept a positional argument — pick whichever the upstream documents as canonical), document the substitution mechanic in the manifest top-of-file rustdoc. The integration test (scope_in #6) and verify script (scope_in #9) use a tmpdir copy of `tests/fixtures/rust-project` (which IS a git repo with commits per the existing g3_plugin_manifests.rs / g4_plugin_manifests.rs fixture-copy pattern). `[capabilities] provides=["platform.git.log", "platform.git.blame", "platform.git.diff"]` (executor: confirm exact tool names from live `tools/list`); `[lifecycle]` mirrors. Top-of-file rustdoc must document: source URL (https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers/tree/main/src/git), pypi package name (`mcp-server-git`), pinned tag, binary launch command, advertised tools (full live list), transport type, API key requirements (NONE — runs against a local git repo only), and the `--repository` placeholder substitution mechanic + rationale.

3. NEW plugin manifest at `plugins/platform/filesystem/plugin.toml` for the official `@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem` upstream MCP server. Required keys: `[plugin] name="filesystem" category="platform" priority="P0" version="<semver>"`; `[transport] type="stdio" command="npx" args=["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem@<pinned-tag>", "<allowed-path-placeholder>"]` — NOTE on the placeholder: the Filesystem MCP server takes one or more allowed-path positional arguments (per upstream README; security-conscious by design — only paths supplied here are accessible). Manifest `args` field carries a placeholder (e.g., `"${UCIL_FS_MCP_ALLOWED_PATH}"` or a documented sentinel) substituted per-invocation; executor confirms upstream's exact argv shape via `--help`. `[capabilities] provides=["platform.fs.read_file", "platform.fs.list_directory", "platform.fs.write_file"]` (executor: confirm exact tool names from live `tools/list`; the `write_file` capability is in the upstream's catalog but tests/verify scripts MUST NOT exercise destructive writes against fixtures — read-only operations only); `[lifecycle]` mirrors. Top-of-file rustdoc must document: source URL (https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers/tree/main/src/filesystem), npm package name, pinned tag, binary launch command, advertised tools (full live list), transport type, API key requirements (NONE — local-only), allowed-path placeholder substitution mechanic, and the read-only-in-tests invariant.

4. NEW devtools script at `scripts/devtools/install-github-mcp.sh`. Idempotent bash; verifies node + npm available; runs `npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-github@<pinned-tag> --help 2>&1 | head -1` (or analogous warm-up that triggers npm fetch + cache without blocking on stdin) to ensure the package is in the npm cache for the integration test's first spawn. Must `set -euo pipefail`; must exit 0 on success; must NOT require sudo; must NOT actually install anything (informational scaffolding only — mirrors `install-context7-mcp.sh` shape verbatim per WO-0074). Document `GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN` env var requirement for full smoke as a `[NOTE]` block at the bottom (informational only — operator-state, NOT actionable by the script).

5. NEW devtools script at `scripts/devtools/install-git-mcp.sh`. Idempotent bash; verifies python3 + uv (or uvx) available (NOT node — Git MCP is pypi-distributed); runs `uvx mcp-server-git@<pinned-tag> --help 2>&1 | head -1` for the warm-up. Mirrors `install-context7-mcp.sh` shape but adapted for uvx. Must NOT install uv if missing (operator-state); EMIT a `[MISSING]` notice if uv/uvx is not on PATH and exit 0 gracefully. Must NOT require sudo. NEW devtools script at `scripts/devtools/install-filesystem-mcp.sh` — mirror `install-github-mcp.sh` (npx-based) shape; pinned package is `@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem@<pinned-tag>`; warm-up via `--help`. Both scripts top-of-file documents the placeholder-substitution mechanic and the read-only-in-tests invariant for filesystem (no `--allowed-path` supplied to warm-up since `--help` doesn't need one).

6. NEW integration-test file at `crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g6_plugin_manifests.rs`. Top of file: mirror existing `g5_plugin_manifests.rs` shape verbatim — same imports, same helper-function placement, same tokio runtime flavor declaration, same `//!` rustdoc declaring mocking is FORBIDDEN. Single `mod g6_plugin_manifests { ... }` block at file root containing THREE `#[tokio::test] async fn` tests — `github_manifest_health_check`, `git_manifest_health_check`, `filesystem_manifest_health_check`. Each test: (a) loads the corresponding `plugins/platform/<name>/plugin.toml` via the daemon's existing manifest loader (use the same APIs as g3/g4/g5_plugin_manifests.rs); (b) substitutes any placeholder tokens in the manifest's transport.args (Git: tmpdir of fixture rust-project; Filesystem: tmpdir of an allow-list-able directory) BEFORE constructing the PluginManager — read the existing g5 test for the substitution helper or implement a small one inline if absent; (c) constructs a `PluginManager` (or analogous abstraction) and calls `health_check_with_timeout` (or the equivalent method that round-trips a real MCP `tools/list` over stdio); (d) asserts the live response carries a specific canonical tool name (e.g. `list_repositories` for GitHub — confirm via live capture; `git_log` for Git — confirm; `read_file` for Filesystem — confirm); (e) panics with `(SAn) <semantic_name>; got: <full-live-tool-list>` on assertion failure per DEC-0007 SA-numbered panic-body format. Substring selectors `g6_plugin_manifests::github_manifest_health_check`, `g6_plugin_manifests::git_manifest_health_check`, `g6_plugin_manifests::filesystem_manifest_health_check` MUST resolve cleanly via `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g6_plugin_manifests <selector>`. NO `mod tests { ... }` nesting; the test functions live at `mod g6_plugin_manifests` ROOT (NOT inside a nested `mod tests` per DEC-0007 + WO-0069/0074 lessons §"For planner"). Mirror the existing `g5_plugin_manifests.rs` file shape verbatim. Provide a `skip_via_env()` helper checking `UCIL_SKIP_PLATFORM_PLUGIN_E2E` per WO-0074 lesson §executor #3 (document inline; default to RUN; verifier MUST NOT set the opt-out per scope_in #14).

7. NEW `scripts/verify/P3-W10-F05.sh` (replace TODO stub) for GitHub MCP. `set -euo pipefail`. (a) Sanity-check `plugins/platform/github/plugin.toml` parses as TOML and asserts the keys named in scope_in #1 via `python3 -c 'import tomllib; ...'`. (b) Run `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g6_plugin_manifests g6_plugin_manifests::github_manifest_health_check 2>&1 | tee /tmp/wo-0075-f05-cargo.log` and `grep -Eq 'test result: ok\. 1 passed; 0 failed|1 tests run: 1 passed' /tmp/wo-0075-f05-cargo.log`. (c) Tool-level smoke (per WO-0074 lesson §executor #4: prefer python polling with deadline over bash sleep): spawn the GitHub MCP server via the canonical `npx` command from the manifest; send an MCP `initialize` + `notifications/initialized` + `tools/list` JSON-RPC sequence over stdio; assert the `tools/list` response is a successful JSON-RPC reply (id matches, no `error` key) and the `tools` array carries ≥3 tools. (d) API-key-gated `tools/call` smoke per WO-0069 Mem0 precedent: `if [ -n "${GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN:-}" ]`, send a `tools/call` for `list_repositories` (or whichever the live catalog confirms is the canonical user-list tool) and assert the response contains a non-error result; ELSE log `[SKIP] tools/call gated on GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN` to stderr and proceed. The cargo-test `tools/list` path is the load-bearing assertion; the API-key-gated tool-level smoke is the additional smoke per F05 spec line `repository file list, PR list, and Actions workflow status queries succeed against a real or mocked GitHub API`. Gate the entire tool-level smoke section behind `UCIL_SKIP_PLATFORM_PLUGIN_E2E` env-var per the WO-0074 precedent — DEFAULT to RUN. Final exit 0 on success; non-zero on any check failure with a clear `>&2` diagnostic.

8. NEW `scripts/verify/P3-W10-F06.sh` (replace TODO stub) for Git MCP. `set -euo pipefail`. (a) TOML sanity check on `plugins/platform/git/plugin.toml`. (b) Cargo-test selector for `g6_plugin_manifests::git_manifest_health_check`. (c) Tool-level smoke: copy `tests/fixtures/rust-project` into `mktemp -d` tmpdir BEFORE invoking the upstream binary (per WO-0074 lesson §executor #5: `DO copy tests/fixtures/rust-project into a mktemp -d tmpdir BEFORE invoking`); spawn `uvx mcp-server-git@<pinned-tag> --repository <tmpdir>` via canonical command; send `initialize` + `notifications/initialized` + `tools/list` + `tools/call` JSON-RPC sequence — `tools/call` invokes `git_log` (or the canonical git-log tool name confirmed from the live catalog) with sensible defaults (e.g., `max_count=5`); assert the response is a successful JSON-RPC reply and the `content` field contains commit-shaped output (heuristic: response text contains a 7+ char hex SHA prefix matching `[0-9a-f]{7,40}` and at least one of `Author` / `Date` / `commit `). (d) Optional second smoke: `tools/call git_diff` against the rust-project's HEAD~1..HEAD (no API key needed). Gate behind `UCIL_SKIP_PLATFORM_PLUGIN_E2E`; DEFAULT to RUN. Final exit 0/non-zero with diagnostics on stderr.

9. NEW `scripts/verify/P3-W10-F07.sh` (replace TODO stub) for Filesystem MCP. `set -euo pipefail`. (a) TOML sanity check on `plugins/platform/filesystem/plugin.toml`. (b) Cargo-test selector for `g6_plugin_manifests::filesystem_manifest_health_check`. (c) Tool-level smoke: create `mktemp -d` tmpdir, populate with 2-3 small text files of known content (DO NOT copy fixtures — fabricate the test inputs in the tmpdir to keep the test hermetic); spawn `npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem@<pinned-tag> <tmpdir>` (passing the tmpdir as the allowed-path argument); send `initialize` + `notifications/initialized` + `tools/list` + `tools/call` JSON-RPC sequence — `tools/call` invokes `read_file` with one of the populated paths and asserts the response content matches the known content; second `tools/call list_directory` against the tmpdir asserts the response lists the populated files. CRITICAL: the smoke MUST be read-only — do NOT exercise any `write_file` or `move_file` upstream tool; the F07 spec line is `secure file read and directory listing work within configured allowed paths`; write operations are out of scope for F07 acceptance. Gate behind `UCIL_SKIP_PLATFORM_PLUGIN_E2E`; DEFAULT to RUN. Final exit 0/non-zero with diagnostics.

10. M1 mutation contract (transport.command poison; runs once per feature, three M1 mutations total). Pre-mutation per feature: `md5sum plugins/platform/<name>/plugin.toml > /tmp/wo-0075-<name>-orig.md5` (capturing snapshot BEFORE any edit). Mutation: edit the targeted `plugin.toml` and replace `command = "npx"` (or `"uvx"` for git) with `command = "/__ucil_test_nonexistent_M1__"`. Selector: the targeted health-check test under `g6_plugin_manifests`. Expected panic: a `PluginError::Spawn { source: Os { code: 2, kind: NotFound } }` chain (or analogous `tokio::process::Command::spawn` ENOENT-class error). Restore: `git checkout -- plugins/platform/<name>/plugin.toml`. Confirm: `md5sum -c /tmp/wo-0075-<name>-orig.md5` returns OK. Mirror the WO-0074 §8 verbatim shape for all three manifests in turn.

11. M2 mutation contract (expected-tool-name regression; runs once per feature, three M2 mutations total). Pre-mutation per feature: `md5sum crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g6_plugin_manifests.rs > /tmp/wo-0075-g6tests-orig.md5` (one snapshot covers all three since the file is shared across tests; verifier may apply mutations sequentially, restoring between each). Mutation: edit the test file and replace whichever string the targeted test asserts as the canonical tool name (e.g. `"list_repositories"` for GitHub, `"git_log"` for Git, `"read_file"` for Filesystem — confirm by reading the test BEFORE picking the literal, since the executor will have selected each from the live `tools/list` response per WO-0074 lesson §executor #1) with `"NON_EXISTENT_TOOL_M2"`. Selector: the targeted test. Expected: structured panic carrying the full live tool-list (`(SAn) <semantic>; got: ["<live tool 1>", "<live tool 2>", ...]; want: "NON_EXISTENT_TOOL_M2"`). Restore: `git checkout -- crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g6_plugin_manifests.rs`. Confirm via md5. Mirror the WO-0074 §9 verbatim shape.

12. Commit cadence: aim for 9-12 atomic commits per `.claude/rules/commit-style.md` ~50 LOC soft target (3× plugin.toml manifests at 60-90 LOC each = candidates for DEC-0005 module-coherence carve-out citation; 3× install scripts at ~70 LOC; 1× integration test file at 250-350 LOC under DEC-0005 carve-out cited in commit body; 3× verify scripts at 200-400 LOC each — F07 will be the smallest, F05 mid-sized due to API-key gating, F06 mid-sized due to fixture-copy + git-log smoke; 1× RFR). Suggested order: (i) plugins/platform/github/plugin.toml + install-github-mcp.sh (paired commit OR two adjacent commits); (ii) plugins/platform/git/plugin.toml + install-git-mcp.sh; (iii) plugins/platform/filesystem/plugin.toml + install-filesystem-mcp.sh; (iv) crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g6_plugin_manifests.rs (one cohesive feat commit covering all three #[tokio::test] async fns under DEC-0005 carve-out — cite in commit body); (v) scripts/verify/P3-W10-F05.sh; (vi) scripts/verify/P3-W10-F06.sh; (vii) scripts/verify/P3-W10-F07.sh; (viii) RFR. Each commit Conventional-Commit-formatted with `Phase: 3` + correct `Feature:` (or all three for shared-infra commits like the integration test file) + `Work-order: WO-0075` trailers.

13. Standing AC23 substantive coverage protocol per WO-0067..WO-0074 carryover (now 31 WOs deep). Use `env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER cargo llvm-cov --package ucil-daemon --tests --summary-only --json | jq '.data[0].totals.lines.percent'` as the authoritative measurement (per WO-0074 lesson §executor: the literal `--no-default-features --features default` arg-pair errors because ucil-daemon has no `default` feature defined). WO is purely additive on tests + manifests + scripts — pre-WO baseline must be preserved (no `crates/ucil-daemon/src/**` source changes per scope_out #2). Document the measurement in the RFR.

14. Apply WO-0074 lessons §"For planner" + §"For executor" + §"For verifier" verbatim: tests at module root (NOT inside `mod tests { ... }`); DEC-0007 SA-numbered panic-body format on every assert; DEC-0008 §4 dependency-inversion seam pattern for any future production-wiring follow-up (NOT in this WO scope — this WO is manifests-only); cite WO-0044 + WO-0069 + WO-0072 + WO-0074 lessons by ID in the RFR's §"Lessons applied" section. Specific WO-0074 lessons to apply verbatim: (a) §executor #1: `DON'T translate live tools/list names to snake_case when they ship kebab-case` — capture canonical names from live binary BEFORE writing assertion literals; (b) §executor #2: `DON'T use --mcp as a devtools warm-up flag` — use `--help` for warm-up; the GitHub/Git/Filesystem MCP servers do not have an `--mcp` flag (they ARE MCP servers by default), so this lesson maps to: avoid invoking the binary in MCP-server mode during warm-up since it would block on stdin; (c) §executor #3: document `UCIL_SKIP_PLATFORM_PLUGIN_E2E` in test rustdoc + all three verify scripts; (d) §executor #4: prefer python polling with deadline over bash sleep for tools/call wall-time-sensitive smokes (F06 git_log + F07 read_file are wall-time-light so bash is fine; F05 GitHub may reach api.github.com over HTTPS — use python with 60-second deadline if the smoke ever times out under bash); (e) §executor #5: `DO copy tests/fixtures/rust-project into a mktemp -d tmpdir BEFORE invoking` — required for F06; (f) §verifier: `Ensure UCIL_SKIP_EXTERNAL_PLUGIN_TESTS and UCIL_SKIP_PLATFORM_PLUGIN_E2E are NOT exported when running cargo test g6_plugin_manifests::* and bash scripts/verify/P3-W10-F0{5,6,7}.sh`.

15. Pre-flight commands the executor MUST run before first push: (a) `cargo build -p ucil-daemon` — clean compile; (b) `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g6_plugin_manifests` — all three frozen tests green; (c) `cargo clippy -p ucil-daemon --all-targets --tests -- -D warnings` — zero warnings; (d) `cargo fmt -p ucil-daemon --check` — formatting clean; (e) word-ban grep on production-side files: `! grep -qiE 'mock|fake|stub' plugins/platform/{github,git,filesystem}/plugin.toml scripts/devtools/install-{github,git,filesystem}-mcp.sh scripts/verify/P3-W10-F0{5,6,7}.sh` returns empty (the `//! Mocking ... is FORBIDDEN` rustdoc declaration in the test file is exempt under WO-0048 line 363 + WO-0072 §executor `Negative assertion forbidding mocking, NOT a mock implementation`); (f) `bash scripts/verify/P3-W10-F05.sh` + `P3-W10-F06.sh` + `P3-W10-F07.sh` all green; (g) `command -v npx uvx python3` confirms PATH has all three required runtimes — the verifier requires the same.

16. Live-tool-name capture protocol (WO-0074 §executor #1 lesson — CRITICAL for M2 mutation correctness): BEFORE writing any tool-name assertion literal, the executor MUST: (a) for GitHub MCP — run `echo '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"wo-0075-tool-list-capture","version":"0.1.0"}}}' | npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-github@<pinned-tag>` (with sufficient stdin frames to drive past initialize → notifications/initialized → tools/list); capture the `tools/list` response's `result.tools[].name` array verbatim; embed this list in the manifest's top-of-file rustdoc comment + the integration test's panic-body string. (b) Repeat for Git MCP via `uvx mcp-server-git@<pinned-tag> --repository /tmp/some-git-repo`. (c) Repeat for Filesystem MCP via `npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem@<pinned-tag> /tmp/some-allowed-dir`. Choose ONE canonical tool name per manifest as the M2 assertion target — prefer the most-stable / most-documented one (e.g., GitHub's `list_repositories` over `search_issues`; Git's `git_log` over `git_show`; Filesystem's `read_file` over `move_file`). Document the chosen literals in the RFR's `## Live tool name capture` section per the WO-0074 RFR template.

17. Capability namespace establishment (`platform.*`): parallel to `search.*` (probe), `navigate.*` (scip), `knowledge.*` (codebase-memory + mem0), `architecture.*` (codegraphcontext), `context.*` (Context7 + Repomix). G6 Platform sources advertise capabilities under the `platform.*` prefix; subdivisions per source: `platform.github.*`, `platform.git.*`, `platform.fs.*`. Subsequent G6 plugins (per master-plan §14.1 line 1678+: Memory MCP, Sequential Thinking MCP, etc.) MUST extend this namespace consistently.
scope_out: 1. NO Aider repo-map (P3-W10-F01) — that's a separate solo WO requiring substantial Rust reimplementation of PageRank over the call graph (master plan §4.5 line 345). This WO is plugin manifests only.

2. NO `crates/ucil-daemon/src/**` source changes. WO is purely additive on `plugins/platform/`, `crates/ucil-daemon/tests/`, `scripts/devtools/`, `scripts/verify/`. Critic precedent at WO-0071 (DEC-0019) flags forbidden_paths violations on daemon-src changes for plugin-manifest-scoped WOs.

3. NO G6 group runtime in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/g6.rs`. That's the WO-0073/0070-equivalent G6 follow-up (G3→G4→G5→G6 template port per WO-0073/0074 lessons §"For planner: G-group recipe") and lands as a separate WO consuming all three plugin manifests from this WO. F08 (G6 Platform aggregation) is the dep-blocked feature ID for that follow-up.
4. NO classify_query / parse_reason daemon-side wiring (deferred since WO-0067/0068). Continues deferred orthogonal to this WO's plugin-manifest scope.

5. NO ADR creation under `ucil-build/decisions/**`. ADRs are planner-territory (DEC-0019 critic precedent at WO-0071 attempt-2). No ambiguity in this WO requires an ADR — the WO-0074 G5 paired-manifest precedent is the direct template.

6. NO modifications to `tests/fixtures/**`. The fixture rust-project + typescript-project are read-only assets — F06 verify script copies into a tmpdir before mutating per scope_in #8; F07 verify script populates a fresh tmpdir from scratch per scope_in #9 (NOT a fixture copy).
7. NO new entries in `feature-list.json`. F05 + F06 + F07 entries already exist with frozen `id` + `description` + `acceptance_tests` + `dependencies` per the no-mid-phase-additions rule.

8. NO P3-W9-F11 (incremental computation integration test) work. Per WO-0073 lessons §"For planner: F11 is orthogonal to G4" — F11 is Salsa-engine + tree-sitter pipeline surface, NOT a context group manifest. Separate WO.
9. NO follow-up daemon spurious-frame-drain code change (DEC-0019 §3 Phase 7 hardening). Unrelated to G6 plugins.
10. NO graphiti P3-W9-F10 plugin manifest. Deferred to Phase 7 per DEC-0019 — not part of this WO.

11. NO G6 aggregation (F08) — depends on F05+F06+F07 first. Lands in the next G6 WO (G6 production-wiring + aggregation, mirroring G4's WO-0073 pattern with `execute_g6` orchestrator + `merge_g6_platform_context` aggregation).
12. NO Memory MCP / Sequential Thinking MCP / other G6 plugins. Each is a separate feature with its own future WO (master-plan §14.1 line 1678+).

13. NO write-side filesystem operations in F07 smoke. The F07 spec line is `secure file read and directory listing` — write_file / move_file / delete are out of scope and MUST NOT be exercised by the verify script even though the upstream Filesystem MCP server advertises them.

14. NO production-wiring of `GitHubG6Source` / `GitG6Source` / `FilesystemG6Source` traits or impls. Those are part of the G6 production-wiring follow-up WO (analogous to G1/G3/G4/G5 production-wiring deferrals per WO-0070/0073/0074 lessons).

15. Effectiveness-gate.sh claude-p sub-session timeout flake (3 open escalations: `20260507T0357Z-effectiveness-nav-rust-symbol-rs-line-flake.md`, `20260507T1629Z-effectiveness-refactor-rename-python-fixture-missing-symbol.md`, `20260507T1930Z-effectiveness-nav-rust-symbol-doctest-caller-flake.md`) — pre-existing standing scope_out, awaiting dedicated harness-improvement WO. WO-0067/0068/0069/0070/0072/0073/0074 carryover, now 8 WOs deep.

16. `scripts/reality-check.sh` pre-existing-stash bug (carries from WO-0067/0068/0069/0070/0072/0073/0074). Workaround unchanged: M1/M2 in-place mutation contract is the authoritative anti-laziness layer; reality-check.sh is informational only.

17. `scripts/verify/coverage-gate.sh` harness improvement (now 31 WOs deep; intermittent — sccache RUSTC_WRAPPER interaction). Continue substantive AC23 coverage check via `env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER cargo llvm-cov` workaround. Bucket B / Bucket D triage candidate. WO-0074 reported the sccache interaction surfaced again; WO-0072 reported clean — intermittent.
acceptance: Three stdio MCP plugin manifests exist under `plugins/platform/` (github, git, filesystem) declaring `platform.*` capability namespace and pinning npm/pypi-package versions for upstream `@modelcontextprotocol/server-github`, `mcp-server-git`, `@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem`.
Three devtools install scripts exist under `scripts/devtools/install-{github,git,filesystem}-mcp.sh` and warm npm/uvx cache for the pinned package versions.

One integration-test file `crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g6_plugin_manifests.rs` declares `mod g6_plugin_manifests { ... }` at file root with three `#[tokio::test] async fn` tests; substring selectors `g6_plugin_manifests::github_manifest_health_check`, `g6_plugin_manifests::git_manifest_health_check`, `g6_plugin_manifests::filesystem_manifest_health_check` resolve cleanly via `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g6_plugin_manifests <selector>`.
All three verify scripts (P3-W10-F05/F06/F07) replace the TODO stubs with real implementations exiting 0 on a green run.

Both M1 (transport.command poison) and M2 (expected-tool-name regression) mutation contracts produce SA-tagged panics with the WO-0074 §8/§9 shape; restore-and-md5-confirm protocol verifies independence — three M1 + three M2 mutations applied per feature.
F05 GitHub MCP API-key short-circuit: `tools/call` smoke gated on `GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN`; `tools/list` cargo-test path is the load-bearing signal regardless of token presence.

F06 Git MCP runs against a tmpdir copy of `tests/fixtures/rust-project`; `tools/call git_log` produces a hex-SHA-prefixed response; F07 Filesystem MCP runs against a freshly-fabricated tmpdir; `tools/call read_file` returns matching content.
No `crates/ucil-daemon/src/**` source changes. No `tests/fixtures/**` modifications. No new feature-list.json entries. No new ADRs.
All commits Conventional-Commit-formatted with `Phase: 3` + correct `Feature:` + `Work-order: WO-0075` trailers.
ucil-daemon line coverage preserved at >= pre-WO baseline (>= 85% floor per master plan §15.4) measured via the `env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER cargo llvm-cov` workaround per WO-0074 standing protocol.
Live tool-name capture documented in the RFR (Section §"Live tool name capture") with the canonical tools/list response from each upstream binary at the chosen pinned version.
acceptance_criteria: python3 -c 'import tomllib; m=tomllib.load(open("plugins/platform/github/plugin.toml","rb")); assert m["plugin"]["name"]=="github"; assert m["plugin"]["category"]=="platform"; assert m["transport"]["type"]=="stdio"; assert any(c.startswith("platform.") for c in m["capabilities"]["provides"])'

python3 -c 'import tomllib; m=tomllib.load(open("plugins/platform/git/plugin.toml","rb")); assert m["plugin"]["name"]=="git"; assert m["plugin"]["category"]=="platform"; assert m["transport"]["type"]=="stdio"; assert any(c.startswith("platform.") for c in m["capabilities"]["provides"])'
